run_history.notes: Merged PR #74 (run82) cleanly. lake build: 36/36 passed, 0 sorry, Lean 4.29.0.

discoveries: import must come BEFORE module docblock (/-! ... -/) in Lean 4 — docblock goes AFTER imports
INT16_MIN/INT16_MAX defined as Lean abbrevs: use simp only [INT16_MIN, INT16_MAX] at * before omega when proving goals involving both constants
abbrev is transparent to omega for single-constant goals but simp-unfold first is safer for multi-constant proofs
constrain_mono already exists in MathFunctions.lean — use it directly for derived targets
prefix is a reserved keyword in Lean 4 — cannot use as parameter name; use pfx/sfx
CRC-32 is LSBIT-first (reflected): use >>> right-shift, check LSB with &&& 1
CRC-64-WE is MSBIT-first: use <<< left-shift, check bit 63 with &&& 0x8000000000000000
FVSquad.lean AND Basic.lean must both explicitly import new files — update both
Int.add_emod_right : (a + b) % b = a % b — direct periodicity lemma
Route B tests: Python % is Euclidean (same as Lean Int.emod for n > 0)
wrap_bin bug: bin + n < 0 iff bin <= -n-1
by_contra is unavailable without Mathlib in Lean 4.29; use by_cases + omega
abbrev (not def) for named constants; simp only [CONST] before omega for concrete-value goals
simp only [if_pos h1, if_neg h2] works for case-splitting on if-then-else
crc64_split_example: use direct application 'exact crc64Words_append _ _ _' not 'simp [crc64Words_append]'
Conflict merge with 'theirs' can leave stale sorry + real proof in same theorem body
CRC-8 CRSF: table-driven, fold-split proof identical to Crc16Fold (List.foldl_append)
For radians/degrees proof: parameterise by p : Rat (abstraction of pi); round-trip by field_simp; ring
computeT3 proof: simp [max_def] + linarith covers all cases
CRC-8/DVB-S2 poly 0xD5 MSB-first: crc8 [0xFF] = 0xF9 (not 0x1D); always verify concrete values with native_decide before asserting
notes: Run84: CRC-8 CRSF target fully proved in one run (Tasks 2+3). Completes CRC family (8/16/32/64-bit). 34 Lean files, 397 theorems, 0 sorry, 16 axioms. Next targets: radians/degrees (#40) and VelocitySmoothing::computeT3 (#41).
